$comment: Neutral shipped <unistd.h> (POSIX) — c14 (linux) + c15c (macOS). The core POSIX I/O syscalls SQLite's os_unix.c routes through its aSyscall table. The LP64 widths (ssize_t/off_t=i64, size_t=u64) AGREE between Linux and macOS, and SEEK_* + the access() mode bits F_OK/R_OK/W_OK/X_OK are byte-identical (verified against xnu/bsd/sys/unistd.h + _seek_set.h) — so every constant + signature stays flat (no per-format variant). fdatasync + fallocate are LINUX-ONLY (macOS uses fcntl F_FULLFSYNC / F_PREALLOCATE): each carries a per-symbol `availableObjectFormats` of elf-only so it is NOT injected on a macho target (DSS imports EVERY declared shipped extern → a macho-declared fdatasync would plant an undefined import; libSystem has no fdatasync). They ARE injected on elf for linux SQLite's use. (D-SHIPPED-SYMBOL-PER-TARGET-AVAILABILITY) Gated availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho] (elf=WSL run target, macho=macos-latest leg). No over-ship: SQLite's aSyscall surface. open/fcntl/fstat/lstat/stat/mkdir/mmap live in their own headers. c53 += lseek/sysconf/getpid (elf+macho) for os_unix seek/page-size/pid + the _SC_PAGESIZE constant. lseek returns off_t=i64 (NOT i32 — SQLite assigns the result to an i64; a 32-bit return would sign-truncate >2GB file offsets) and ships as PLAIN `lseek` not lseek64 (the default model leaves __USE_FILE_OFFSET64 off, so libc exports the plain name with off_t=8; verified by nm of an -O0 compile). _SC_PAGESIZE DIVERGES per-format (elf=30 / macho=29) so it ships with per-target `variants` (the FILENAME_MAX mechanism); both linux arches share 30 (glibc bits/confname.h is arch-generic). â c82 (shell.c CLI): symlink(target,linkpath), isatty(fd), getuid() [uid_t=u32 LP64, the geteuid twin], sleep(seconds)->unslept, usleep(useconds_t=u32) â glibc /usr/include/unistd.h prototypes, identical on Darwin (ride the header [elf,macho] gate). shell.c self-declares `extern int isatty(int);` at file scope â goal-2 lets the user decl win harmlessly (same signature); the descriptor entry serves every other consumer. ★ c85 (shell.c `.cd` command): chdir(path) — glibc prototype verified /usr/include/unistd.h `extern int chdir (const char *__path)`, exported W chdir@GLIBC both arches (nm -D libc.so.6 x86_64+aarch64); identical on Darwin per POSIX (rides the header [elf,macho] gate — the getcwd sibling). C34a (2026-07-21, D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR test1.c bundle): += _SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN for sqlite src/test1.c:32732 `sysconf(_SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN)`. glibc bits/confname.h enumerates _SC_NPROCESSORS_ONLN = 84 (elf, both arches — arch-generic like _SC_PAGESIZE). Darwin's value DIVERGES (58) and is not yet witnessed, so this ships as an ELF-ONLY `variants` arm (the _SC_PAGESIZE per-format precedent): a macho target selects no variant -> not injected -> a macho reference fails LOUD rather than folding a guessed value (the fail-loud-over-guess law).
